question: If 5 workers can build 100 m of road in 8 days, how many metres can the same workers build in 16 days?
explanation: Time doubles while workers remain the same, so work also doubles. Therefore, road built = 100 × (16/8) = 200 m.
shortcut: If only time changes, work changes directly with time.
learning_tip: Whenever only one quantity changes, check whether it is directly or inversely proportional.

question: If 8 workers complete a job in 12 days, how many days will 16 workers take to complete the same job?
explanation: Workers and days are inversely proportional. Days = 12 × (8/16) = 6 days.
learning_tip: Workers and time always have an inverse relationship for the same work.

question: 12 men can complete a work in 15 days. How many men are needed to complete the same work in 9 days?
learning_tip: Increase in workers decreases the required time.

question: 15 machines produce 900 items in 6 hours. How many items will 20 machines produce in the same time?
explanation: Production is directly proportional to the number of machines. Items = 900 × (20/15) = 1200.
learning_tip: Keep time constant before applying direct proportion.

question: A car travels 240 km in 4 hours. At the same speed, how far will it travel in 7 hours?
explanation: Distance is directly proportional to time. Distance = 240 × (7/4) = 420 km.
shortcut: Same speed ⇒ Distance changes directly with time.
learning_tip: Whenever speed is constant, use direct proportion.

question: 8 workers working 6 hours a day complete a task in 10 days. How many days will 12 workers working 8 hours a day take?
shortcut: Multiply quantities that increase work and divide accordingly.
learning_tip: Chain Rule combines multiple direct and inverse proportions into one equation.

question: If 4 pumps fill a tank in 15 hours, how many hours will 6 similar pumps take?
explanation: Pumps and time are inversely proportional. Time = 15 × (4/6) = 10 hours.
learning_tip: Treat pumps like workers in chain rule problems.

question: 18 workers working 8 hours per day finish a project in 20 days. How many workers are required to finish the same project in 16 days by working 9 hours per day?
learning_tip: This is one of the most frequently asked Chain Rule questions in placement exams.

question: 20 workers working 6 hours a day complete a job in 18 days. How many workers are required to complete the same job in 12 days by working 9 hours a day?
learning_tip: When work is constant, workers are directly proportional and days/hours are inversely proportional.

question: 12 machines manufacture 720 units in 8 hours. How many units will 18 machines manufacture in the same time?
shortcut: Only machines change, so use direct proportion.
learning_tip: If time remains constant, production depends only on the number of machines.

question: A train covers 480 km in 8 hours. At the same speed, how much distance will it cover in 15 hours?
shortcut: Distance changes directly with time when speed is constant.
learning_tip: Keep speed constant before applying direct proportion.

question: 24 workers complete a project in 30 days. After 10 days, 12 workers leave. In how many more days will the remaining work be completed?
explanation: Total work = 24 × 30 = 720 worker-days. Work done in 10 days = 24 × 10 = 240 worker-days. Remaining work = 480 worker-days. Remaining workers = 12. Days = 480 ÷ 12 = 40.
shortcut: Convert the work into worker-days first.
learning_tip: Worker-day is the easiest way to solve workers leaving/joining questions.

question: 16 workers working 9 hours per day complete a job in 15 days. How many workers working 6 hours per day are needed to complete the same work in 18 days?
learning_tip: Chain Rule questions usually combine multiple proportional relationships.

question: 6 identical pumps fill a reservoir in 20 hours. If 2 pumps stop after 5 hours, how many additional hours are required to fill the reservoir?
explanation: Total work = 6 × 20 = 120 pump-hours. Work completed in first 5 hours = 6 × 5 = 30 pump-hours. Remaining work = 90 pump-hours. Remaining pumps = 4. Time = 90 ÷ 4 = 22.5 hours.
learning_tip: Pump questions are solved exactly like worker problems.

question: 15 workers working 8 hours daily complete a project in 24 days. How many workers working 6 hours daily are required to complete the project in 20 days?
learning_tip: This is one of the most common placement questions based on the Chain Rule.

question: 10 machines working 12 hours daily manufacture 18,000 units in 15 days. How many machines working 10 hours daily are needed to manufacture 30,000 units in 20 days?
learning_tip: When production also changes, include it in the chain proportion equation.

question: 18 workers working 10 hours a day complete a project in 12 days. How many workers working 8 hours a day are required to complete the same project in 15 days?
learning_tip: Whenever work is constant, use the product of workers, hours and days.

question: 15 machines produce 4,500 units in 5 days by working 8 hours daily. How many machines are needed to produce 9,000 units in 6 days by working 10 hours daily?
shortcut: Machines are directly proportional to production and inversely proportional to hours and days.
learning_tip: Always arrange the quantities before substituting values.

question: 30 men can complete a work in 24 days. After 8 days, 6 more men join them. In how many more days will the remaining work be completed?
explanation: Total work = 30 × 24 = 720 man-days. Work done in 8 days = 240 man-days. Remaining work = 480 man-days. Remaining workers = 36. Days = 480 ÷ 36 = 13.33 ≈ 14 days.
shortcut: Convert the work into man-days before solving.
learning_tip: Man-day is the easiest method for workers joining or leaving.

question: A vehicle travels 360 km in 6 hours. At the same speed, how long will it take to travel 660 km?
shortcut: At constant speed, time is directly proportional to distance.
learning_tip: When speed remains constant, use direct proportion.

question: 24 workers working 9 hours daily complete a project in 16 days. How many workers working 12 hours daily can complete the same project in 12 days?
learning_tip: Always multiply all quantities before dividing.

question: 20 machines working 8 hours daily manufacture 16,000 units in 10 days. How many machines working 10 hours daily are required to manufacture 30,000 units in 12 days?
learning_tip: Production questions are direct applications of the Chain Rule.

question: 12 workers working 7 hours daily complete a task in 30 days. How many workers working 9 hours daily are needed to complete the task in 20 days?
learning_tip: This model appears frequently in placement aptitude tests.

question: 18 workers working 8 hours daily complete 2,400 units in 15 days. How many workers working 10 hours daily are required to produce 4,000 units in 18 days?
shortcut: Include production along with workers, hours and days in one equation.
learning_tip: These mixed production questions are common in TCS, Infosys and Accenture exams.

question: 16 workers working 9 hours a day can complete a project in 18 days. If the work is increased by 25% and each worker now works 8 hours a day, how many workers are required to complete the new project in 20 days?
explanation:
New work = 125% of original work. Using the chain rule:
Workers = (16 × 9 × 18 × 125) ÷ (20 × 8 × 100) = 20.25 ≈ 20 workers.
shortcut: Multiply by the work increase factor before applying the chain rule formula.
learning_tip: This is one of the highest-level Chain Rule questions asked in placement tests. Identify which quantities are directly proportional (work) and which are inversely proportional (workers, hours, days) before forming the equation.
